Uplink

Computer-Assisted Reporting Newsletter, 1990–2007

Browse Topics Search

Uplink update

April 1995
EducationInvestigative MethodsPublic Safety

This journalism newsletter focuses on computer-assisted reporting techniques for education beat coverage, featuring expert journalists discussing data analysis methods for stories on segregation and test scores. It also includes guidance on using data to investigate regulatory enforcement issues and accessing federal information online.

School is in. The education beat is made for computer-assisted reporting. In this issue, top journalists from Florida, New Jersey and Washington, D.C, tell you everything from analyzing segregation to SAT scores. These stories underscore how reporters can improve their coverage day in and day out by knowing how to handle electronic information.

In addition, Dateline NBC's David Hinchman recounts how he battled for data to show how the FAA doesn't enforce its rules, and how commuter airline safety suffers.

And we help you keep abreast of what's happening on the Internet, including tips on how to check the federal budget on line.
